canonical_definition: The construct of Security is framed in two distinct ways across the provided literature. One paper defines it broadly as a model's "resistance to generating harmful or malicious content," positioning security as a property whose implications in areas like LLM quantization are "largely unexplored." A different and more specific usage treats security as "the resilience of a watermarking scheme against attacks aimed at reverse-engineering, stealing, or illegitimately applying the watermark rules." In this context, security is presented as one component in a trade-off with robustness and text quality. This framing is concerned with vulnerabilities like "watermark stealing," where an adversary might reverse-engineer the system. The provided data does not specify any measurement instruments or tasks used to operationalize this construct.
papers: CanLLMs Evaluate Complex Attribution inQA? Automatic Benchmarking using Knowledge Graphs
AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
OccamLLM: Fast and Exact Language Model Arithmetic in a Single Step

definitions.text: The latent property of a model regarding its resistance to generating harmful or malicious content.

relationships.paper: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
